headerSubtitle: D.M.A.  |  U.S. Army Veteran (20 Years)  |  Active Federal Secret Clearance
summary: Retired 20-year U.S. Army veteran and Washington Vets-to-Tech graduate moving into technical program management. For twenty years, a full parallel career in operations, personnel security records, and curriculum leadership meant coordinating people, schedules, and compliance requirements at scale, the same discipline a technical program manager applies to cross-functional delivery. Two Vets-to-Tech certificate programs, an AWS Cloud Practitioner certification, and an active Secret-level clearance bring that leadership record into direct contact with the technical work it will manage.
honestPositioningNote: Tim has not held a formal program or product manager title. What transfers directly, and is fully verified, is large-scale operations coordination, personnel records and compliance administration, curriculum design across multiple instructor teams, and sustained readiness metrics. Applications built from this profile should frame the transfer as leadership and coordination experience applied to technical programs, not as prior TPM tenure. No PM-specific tooling (Jira, Asana, etc.) should be claimed beyond what's in USER_PROFILE.md's honest qualifiers.

coreCompetencies.program_operations: Cross-team coordination at scale (40-person organization), resource and equipment inventory management, sustained readiness and quality standards across 400-plus recurring deliverables
coreCompetencies.compliance_and_records: Personnel security clearance records administration (40-plus records), HR and library records management across international assignments, strict data-privacy/compliance environment experience (DOL internship)
coreCompetencies.curriculum_and_program_design: Curriculum design and delivery to audiences with widely differing levels of preparation, team contribution to a 58% training-cycle-time reduction, individual remediation planning and blind dual-assessor evaluation design
coreCompetencies.technical_foundation: AWS EC2 deployment, Windows Server/PowerShell, Active Directory & Group Policy, Linux/Bash, Python scripting (~1 year), Ansible automation, security hardening and vulnerability assessment fundamentals

certifications.name: CompTIA Security+
certifications.note: Exam scheduled July 30, 2026, McChord testing center, 12:30pm. Satisfies DoD 8570/8140 IAT Level II upon passing.

experience.title: Information Services Intern
bullets: Operated within a strict data-privacy and compliance environment, logging and tracking service requests in ServiceNow per agency ISD policy.
Supported account management, software configuration, and workstation setup/imaging for agency staff.

experience.title: IT Trainee — Cybersecurity
bullets: Completed a 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science, with a 100% lab completion rate across security hardening, threat intelligence, vulnerability assessment, and cloud technologies.

experience.title: IT Trainee — Server & Cloud Applications
bullets: Completed a 16-week intensive program earning 18 college credits toward a B.S. in Computer Science, with a 100% lab completion rate across Linux, Windows Server, networking, AWS, and Python scripting.
Led a solo capstone project provisioning a VM via Vagrant with an Ansible playbook customized from an instructor-provided template.

experience.title: Production Manager / Library, Security & Operations Supervisor
bullets: Coordinated a 40-person organization across 400-plus public events and an audience reach of 2.5 million-plus, sustaining 100% readiness and managing a large equipment inventory.
Administered personnel security clearance records for 40-plus team members.
Delivered resilience and performance training to 200 personnel across 10 cohorts as a certified Level 2 facilitator.

experience.title: Lead Instructor / Curriculum Writer
bullets: Contributed as part of a curriculum team to a 58% reduction in training cycle time, maintaining 80 personnel records and coaching 12 teams.
Advised 20 mid-level and 40 senior leaders and assessed 400 student performances across audiences with widely differing levels of preparation.

Designed individual remediation plans and a blind dual-assessor evaluation process for at-risk students; achieved a 100% pass rate against the program's full testing standard. Distinguished Leader Award, Army Leadership Development Program, 2010.

experience.title: Library & HR Records Supervisor / Saxophonist
bullets: Managed HR records and asset documentation for 40-plus personnel across international assignments; deployed in support of Operation Iraqi Freedom (2007).

projects.name: SIP — Systematic Instrumental Practice
projects.description: In-progress Android-first Flutter application for instrumental musicians using FSRS-5 scheduling, a Python/FastAPI backend, and py-fsrs. Grounded in doctoral research applying Edwin Gordon's Music Learning Theory.
projects.relevance: Ran a multi-model AI design council (Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Perplexity) to reach a canonical design specification, evidence of coordinating multiple inputs toward one technical decision, a core TPM function, not a claim of shipped commercial software.

projects.name: Vagrant + Ansible VM Provisioning (WaV2T Capstone)
projects.description: Solo capstone project provisioning a single VM via Vagrant, using an Ansible playbook customized from an instructor-provided template.
projects.relevance: Concrete, named project anchor for infrastructure-as-code exposure.

additional: Maintains website, CMS, and social media (WordPress, Canva, Bluehost, Google Forms) and runs live sound board operations for a community theatre organization, ongoing
